all.en: On Sunday morning, the weekend flea market was already buzzing with energy.
all.zh: 週日早晨，週末跳蚤市場已經充滿了熱鬧的氛圍。

all.en: Pei-shan set up her small table under a colorful umbrella.
all.zh: 佩珊在一把色彩繽紛的傘下架起她的小桌子。

all.en: She had spent the whole week sorting through old items she no longer needed.
all.zh: 她花了整整一週整理自己不再需要的舊物。

all.en: There were vintage books, second-hand clothes, kitchen tools, and even a guitar.
all.zh: 有古董書、二手衣物、廚房用品，甚至還有一把吉他。

all.en: A young couple bargained politely for a wooden picture frame she had labeled at two hundred dollars.
all.zh: 一對年輕情侶禮貌地為一個她標價兩百元的木製相框議價。

all.en: After a friendly conversation, she agreed to sell it for one hundred and fifty.
all.zh: 友善地交談一番後，她同意以一百五十元賣出。

all.en: A teenager bought the guitar and promised to learn his first song that very night.
all.zh: 一位青少年買下了那把吉他，並承諾當晚就要學會第一首歌。

all.en: An old gentleman flipped through her books and shared stories about each one.
all.zh: 一位老先生翻閱她的書籍，並分享了每本書相關的故事。

all.en: By noon, she had sold more than half of her items and earned three thousand dollars.
all.zh: 到中午時，她已經賣出了一半以上的物品，賺了三千元。

all.en: It felt strange to see her old belongings go home with new owners.
all.zh: 看著自己的舊物跟新主人回家的感覺很奇妙。

all.en: Pei-shan realized that one person's clutter could truly be another person's treasure.
all.zh: 佩珊明白了一個人的雜物確實可以是另一個人的寶藏。

all.en: After lunch, the afternoon crowd grew even bigger as families wandered between the colorful stalls.
all.zh: 午餐過後，下午的人潮變得更多，一家大小在色彩繽紛的攤位之間閒逛。

all.en: A little girl pointed at a stuffed bear and begged her mother to buy it.
all.zh: 一個小女孩指著一隻絨毛熊，央求媽媽買下它。

all.en: Pei-shan decided to give the bear to the child for free as a kind gesture.
all.zh: 佩珊決定免費把熊送給那個孩子，作為一份善意的表達。

all.en: The mother insisted on paying something and finally placed two hundred dollars in her hand.
all.zh: 那位母親堅持要付一些錢，最後在她手中放了兩百元。

all.en: By three o'clock, dark clouds began to gather above the open-air market.
all.zh: 到了三點鐘，烏雲開始在露天市集上方聚集。

all.en: Nearby vendors quickly helped each other cover their goods with waterproof plastic sheets.
all.zh: 附近的攤販迅速地互相幫忙用防水塑膠布蓋住商品。

all.en: The sudden shower lasted only fifteen minutes before the warm sun returned.
all.zh: 突如其來的陣雨只持續了十五分鐘，溫暖的陽光便重新出現。

all.en: A neighboring vendor offered her hot tea and shared homemade rice cakes during the break.
all.zh: 鄰攤的攤販在休息時請她喝熱茶，並分享自製的米糕。

all.en: When closing time approached, she packed her remaining items into two light boxes.
all.zh: 接近收攤時間時，她把剩下的物品打包進兩個輕便的紙箱裡。

all.en: On the way home, she already planned to return next month with even more treasures.
all.zh: 回家的路上，她已經計畫下個月帶著更多的寶藏再來一次。

all.en: The next morning, Pei-shan inspected her unsold items and chose a fresh display arrangement.
all.zh: 隔天早上，佩珊檢視了未賣出的物品，並挑選了一種全新的展示擺設方式。

all.en: She washed the second-hand clothes carefully and ironed them to look more attractive to buyers.
all.zh: 她仔細地清洗了二手衣物，並燙得平整，讓它們對買家更有吸引力。

all.en: Her cousin offered to help next weekend in exchange for sharing a small portion of profits.
all.zh: 她的表妹提議下個週末過來幫忙，條件是分享一小部分的利潤。

all.en: Together, they painted a wooden sign with bright colors to attract more curious passersby.
all.zh: 她們一起用鮮豔的顏色彩繪了一塊木製招牌，以吸引更多好奇的路人。

all.en: On the second outing, regular customers recognized her stall and brought friends along to shop.
all.zh: 第二次擺攤時，老顧客認出了她的攤位，並帶著朋友一起來購物。

all.en: A foreign tourist asked thoughtful questions about each handmade item before making a careful selection.
all.zh: 一位外國觀光客在仔細挑選之前，針對每件手工藝品提出了用心的問題。

all.en: Pei-shan practiced simple English phrases to describe materials, prices and the history behind certain pieces.
all.zh: 佩珊練習了簡單的英文短句，用來描述材質、價格以及某些物品背後的歷史。

all.en: By early afternoon, she had nearly doubled her earnings compared to her very first market day.
all.zh: 到了下午稍早，她的收入已將近是她第一次擺攤那天的兩倍。

all.en: She decided to donate ten percent of her profits to a local animal shelter every month.
all.zh: 她決定每個月把利潤的百分之十捐給在地的動物收容所。

all.en: What began as cleaning out clutter slowly grew into a meaningful little weekend business venture.
all.zh: 原本只是清理雜物的活動，慢慢演變成一項有意義的小型週末生意。

all.en: News of her shelter donations spread quickly through the local online community group.
all.zh: 她捐助收容所的消息很快在當地的線上社群中傳開了。

all.en: A reporter from a community newspaper interviewed Pei-shan about her unusual weekend business journey.
all.zh: 一位社區報的記者採訪了佩珊，談談她不尋常的週末創業歷程。

all.en: The published article attracted many new customers who specifically came to support her kind cause.
all.zh: 刊出的文章吸引了許多新顧客，他們特地前來支持她的善舉。

all.en: She gradually started taking small custom orders for handmade gifts from her growing customer base.
all.zh: 她漸漸開始接受日益增多的顧客所下的手工禮品小型訂製訂單。

all.en: To manage demand, Pei-shan opened a simple online shop on a popular Taiwanese marketplace platform.
all.zh: 為了應付需求，佩珊在一個熱門的台灣電商平台上開了一家簡易網路商店。

all.en: Her younger sister offered to help photograph products and write friendly item descriptions in Chinese.
all.zh: 她妹妹主動幫忙拍攝商品照片，並用中文撰寫親切的商品說明。

all.en: They turned the spare bedroom into a small workshop with proper shelves and a sewing corner.
all.zh: 她們把空房改造成一個小工作室，擺上適當的層架和一個縫紉角落。

all.en: By the end of the year, the animal shelter received its largest single donation from her shop.
all.zh: 到年底時，那間動物收容所收到了來自她店裡單筆最大的捐款。

all.en: The shelter director invited Pei-shan to become an official monthly volunteer and community partner.
all.zh: 收容所所長邀請佩珊成為正式的每月志工，也成為社區合作夥伴。

all.en: What started as decluttering had quietly transformed into a heartfelt mission that helped both animals and people.
all.zh: 原本只是斷捨離的行動，悄悄轉變成一項真誠的使命，同時幫助了動物與人。

all.en: Six months later, Pei-shan's small workshop produced enough handmade goods to require a dedicated assistant.
all.zh: 六個月後，佩珊的小工作室產出的手工商品多到需要一位專屬助理。

all.en: She hired a quiet art student who needed flexible hours around her demanding university course schedule.
all.zh: 她雇用了一位安靜的藝術系學生，對方需要能配合她繁重大學課程的彈性工時。

all.en: Together they experimented with eco-friendly packaging made from recycled paper and natural fabric scraps.
all.zh: 她們一起試驗用回收紙和天然布料碎片製成的環保包裝。

all.en: Customers loved the thoughtful packaging and often kept the small fabric bags for their own daily use.
all.zh: 顧客喜愛這份用心的包裝，經常把小布袋留下來自己日常使用。

all.en: Pei-shan was invited to give a short talk at a small Taipei sustainable design conference.
all.zh: 佩珊受邀在台北一場小型的永續設計研討會中發表簡短演講。

all.en: On stage, she shared her journey from cluttered apartment to meaningful weekend business with honest humor.
all.zh: 在台上，她以真誠又幽默的方式分享了從雜亂公寓到有意義週末事業的歷程。

all.en: Several university students approached her afterward, eager to learn how to start similar local projects.
all.zh: 事後好幾位大學生上前找她，渴望學習如何開始類似的在地計畫。

all.en: She decided to host monthly free workshops teaching basic upcycling skills in the animal shelter's spare room.
all.zh: 她決定每月在動物收容所的空閒房間舉辦免費工作坊，教導基本的舊物再造技巧。

all.en: The shelter benefited because every workshop attendee donated a small fee directly to support the rescued animals.
all.zh: 收容所因此受惠，因為每位工作坊參加者都直接捐了一小筆費用支援受救援的動物。

all.en: What had once been ordinary clutter quietly became a thriving cycle of giving, creating and community building.
all.zh: 曾經只是平凡的雜物，悄悄變成了一個給予、創造與社區建構的蓬勃循環。

all.en: By the second spring, Pei-shan's small online shop welcomed orders from three quiet neighboring countries.
all.zh: 到了第二個春天，佩珊的小型網路商店收到了來自三個鄰近寧靜國家的訂單。

all.en: She carefully translated her item descriptions with patient help from her growing volunteer customer community.
all.zh: 她在日益壯大的志工顧客社群耐心協助下，仔細翻譯了她的商品說明。

all.en: A retired English teacher in Japan offered weekly free editing in exchange for a small fabric pouch.
all.zh: 日本一位退休英文老師每週提供免費校稿，只要求收到一個小布包作為交換。

all.en: Pei-shan sewed her a special pouch using carefully chosen indigo cloth from a Tainan vintage market.
all.zh: 佩珊用台南古物市場精心挑選的藍染布，為她縫製了一個特別的布包。

all.en: The teacher mailed back a tender thank-you photograph holding the pouch beside her elderly garden cat.
all.zh: 那位老師寄回一張溫柔的感謝照片，懷裡抱著布包，旁邊是她年邁的庭院貓咪。

all.en: Pei-shan framed the photograph above her sewing machine to remind herself of her quiet global friends.
all.zh: 佩珊把照片裱起來掛在縫紉機上方，提醒自己那些安靜的全球朋友。

all.en: The animal shelter expanded its kitten room thanks to her growing monthly contribution and gentle visitors.
all.zh: 由於她不斷增加的月捐和溫柔來訪者，動物收容所擴建了幼貓房。

all.en: She brought her young niece every weekend to gently brush kittens and quietly read storybooks aloud.
all.zh: 她每個週末都帶著小姪女去，溫柔地幫小貓梳毛，並輕聲朗讀故事書。

all.en: Her niece adopted one shy black kitten and named her Petal after a favorite garden flower.
all.zh: 她的姪女領養了一隻害羞的黑色幼貓，以她最愛的庭院花朵為牠取名「花瓣」。

all.en: What began as decluttering had quietly stitched together a soft web of kindness across oceans and homes.
all.zh: 原本只是斷捨離，已悄悄編織出一張溫柔的善意之網，橫跨海洋與許多家庭。

all.en: By autumn, Pei-shan invited her grandmother to share traditional embroidery skills with the workshop attendees.
all.zh: 到了秋天，佩珊邀請奶奶到工作坊與學員們分享傳統刺繡技藝。

all.en: Her grandmother arrived nervously, carrying a small wooden box of threads collected since her own girlhood.
all.zh: 奶奶緊張地到場，手裡捧著一只小木盒，裡頭裝著她少女時期收集至今的繡線。

all.en: Students listened quietly as she demonstrated her mother's gentle stitch passed down across three quiet generations.
all.zh: 學員們安靜聆聽，她示範著母親傳下、靜靜流傳三代的溫柔針法。

all.en: She left that evening with bright eyes and a promise to return every month if her health allowed.
all.zh: 那天傍晚她帶著明亮的眼神離開，並承諾只要身體許可，每個月都會再來。

all.en: The next workshop sold out within hours after word spread about her tender, patient teaching style.
all.zh: 下一場工作坊在她溫柔耐心的教學風格傳開後，幾小時內就額滿了。

all.en: Pei-shan recorded short videos of her grandmother's hands working slowly across the soft cotton cloth.
all.zh: 佩珊錄下了奶奶雙手緩緩在柔軟棉布上穿梭的短影片。

all.en: The videos reached quiet elderly viewers who had also longed to revisit half-forgotten childhood needlework habits.
all.zh: 那些影片傳到了安靜的長輩觀眾手中，他們也渴望重溫早已半遺忘的童年針線習慣。

all.en: One viewer in Yilan mailed her grandmother's old thimble as a quiet token of shared heritage.
all.zh: 宜蘭的一位觀眾寄來了她奶奶的舊頂針，作為共享傳承的靜默信物。

all.en: Pei-shan's grandmother kept the thimble inside her wooden thread box beside her own mother's small scissors.
all.zh: 佩珊的奶奶把那枚頂針放進她的木製繡線盒，緊鄰著自己母親留下的小剪刀。

all.en: Three generations of quiet hands now stitched together inside one small box of patient memory.
all.zh: 三代靜謐的雙手，如今在這個盛裝耐心回憶的小盒裡，一同縫合著時光。

all.en: Before winter arrived, Pei-shan organized a small reunion of her loyal customers and weekend market neighbors.
all.zh: 冬天來臨前，佩珊舉辦了一場小型聚會，邀請忠實顧客與週末市集的攤友們。

all.en: Each guest brought one secondhand item with a meaningful, gently handwritten story tied around the handle.
all.zh: 每位客人都帶來一件二手物品，並把一段親手寫下、意義深遠的故事繫在把手上。

all.en: They exchanged the items in a quiet circle, each story read aloud by its previous patient owner.
all.zh: 他們在安靜的圓圈裡交換物品，每段故事都由原本耐心的主人輕聲朗讀。

all.en: An old gardener gave away his late wife's small watering can to a quiet young new bride.
all.zh: 一位老園丁把亡妻的小灑水壺，送給一位安靜的年輕新嫁娘。

all.en: She cried quietly and promised to water her new balcony herbs with deep, daily gratitude.
all.zh: 她靜靜地落淚，承諾會以深深的每日感恩之心澆灌她陽台上的新香草。

all.en: Pei-shan recorded every traded item and story into a small leather album bound by her cousin.
all.zh: 佩珊把每件交換的物品與故事，記錄在一本由表妹親手裝訂的小皮革相簿裡。

all.en: The album rested beside her sewing machine, growing thicker after every quiet seasonal gathering held.
all.zh: 那本相簿擺在縫紉機旁，每一場安靜的季節聚會後都變得更加厚實。

all.en: Visitors often flipped through the pages while sipping tea, smiling at strangers they had never met.
all.zh: 訪客常邊啜飲茶水邊翻閱書頁，對著從未謀面的陌生人露出微笑。

all.en: A patient flea market table had grown into a long chain of strangers gently passing meaning forward.
all.zh: 一張耐心的跳蚤市場攤桌，已成長為一條長長的陌生人之鏈，溫柔地把意義往前傳遞。

all.en: Pei-shan smiled at the album, knowing every gentle weekend sale had quietly sewn a wider kinder world.
all.zh: 佩珊望著相簿微笑，知道每一個溫柔的週末擺攤，都悄悄縫合出一個更寬廣、更溫暖的世界。

all.en: Last Saturday morning, Pei-shan woke up to heavy rain hammering against her small studio window.
all.zh: 上週六早上，佩珊被打在小工作室窗戶上的大雨吵醒。

all.en: She nearly cancelled the flea market until her assistant texted that customers were already waiting.
all.zh: 她差點要取消擺攤，直到助理傳訊息說已經有顧客在等了。

all.en: They quickly packed the van with extra plastic sheets, sturdy umbrellas, and warm flasks of barley tea.
all.zh: 她們匆忙把多餘的塑膠布、堅固的雨傘和保溫瓶裝的麥茶塞進廂型車。

all.en: When they arrived, three regular customers helped them set up the tent before even saying hello.
all.zh: 抵達時，三位熟客還沒打招呼就先過來幫她們搭帳棚。

all.en: An elderly man brought her a hot scallion pancake wrapped neatly in brown paper from a nearby stall.
all.zh: 一位老先生從附近攤位買了一份熱蔥油餅，用牛皮紙仔細包好遞給她。

all.en: Despite the rain, families wandered between covered stalls and laughed at puddles forming under cracked umbrellas.
all.zh: 儘管下著雨，一家家人仍在有遮雨棚的攤位間穿梭，對著破傘下形成的小水窪大笑。

all.en: A young art student bought her last embroidered handkerchief as a graduation gift for her mother.
all.zh: 一位年輕的藝術系學生買走了她最後一條刺繡手帕，作為送給媽媽的畢業禮物。

all.en: By noon, she had earned more than on any sunny weekend before, much to her quiet surprise.
all.zh: 到中午，她賺得比以往任何一個晴朗週末都多，讓她暗暗驚喜。

all.en: She shared her earnings with her assistant and donated the extra amount to the animal shelter on the spot.
all.zh: 她把收入分給助理，並當場把多出來的那部分捐給了動物收容所。

all.en: On the drive home, the rain finally softened and a small bright rainbow appeared above the river.
all.zh: 回程路上，雨終於小了，一道明亮的小彩虹出現在河面上。

all.en: Her assistant snapped a photo from the passenger window for the next page of their leather album.
all.zh: 助理從副駕駛座窗口拍下那張照片，要放進皮革相簿的下一頁。

all.en: Pei-shan smiled, knowing even the rainiest market days could quietly become some of the warmest pages.
all.zh: 佩珊微笑著，知道連最陰雨的擺攤日，也能悄悄成為相簿裡最溫暖的幾頁。

all.en: I priced each item clearly so buyers would not need to ask.
all.zh: 我把每件商品都清楚標價,讓買家不必開口問。

all.en: An old camera sold within the first ten minutes of the morning.
all.zh: 一台舊相機在早上頭十分鐘內就賣掉了。

all.en: Customers love to bargain, so I left a little room in my prices.
all.zh: 客人喜歡殺價,所以我在訂價時留了一點空間。

all.en: Arranging the table neatly drew far more curious shoppers.
all.zh: 把攤位擺得整齊,吸引了多得多的好奇顧客。

all.en: A friendly smile sold more than any clever sales pitch.
all.zh: 一個親切的微笑,比任何高明的推銷話術都更會賣東西。

all.en: I traded a chipped vase for a cup of hot tea next door.
all.zh: 我用一個有缺口的花瓶,跟隔壁攤換了一杯熱茶。

all.en: Bringing small change made every transaction quick and easy.
all.zh: 準備好零錢,讓每筆交易都又快又順。

all.en: By afternoon, my crowded table had become pleasantly empty.
all.zh: 到了下午,我原本擁擠的攤位已經令人開心地空了出來。

all.en: Some buyers shared stories about why they wanted my old books.
all.zh: 有些買家分享他們為什麼想要我那些舊書的故事。

all.en: Clearing out clutter and earning cash felt doubly rewarding.
all.zh: 既清掉了雜物又賺到現金,感覺加倍值得。
